developer_constraints: CPU-only inference, mixed-plane (sagittal, coronal, axial) uncurated MRI scans without metadata.

preprocessing_strategy.action: Geometric Extreme-Point Cropping & Soft CLAHE (8x8 tiles).
preprocessing_strategy.justification: Bypassed standard skull-stripping (like FSL BET) because it frequently misclassifies and strips peripheral Meningiomas. Used Otsu's thresholding for bounding-box crops to remove dead space, followed by CLAHE to mathematically preserve the high-frequency edge gradients of diffuse tumors.

classical_pipeline_features.components: GLCM (5 macro-texture), LBP (26 micro-patterns), Global Stats (4), and an 8x8 Spatial Grid (128 localized intensity stats).
classical_pipeline_features.justification: The 8x8 spatial grid acts like a local receptive field, preventing the global metrics from washing out the signal of small, localized tumors.

models_and_performance.primary_classifier: Support Vector Machine (RBF Kernel, C=1.0). Achieved 88.19% Test Accuracy and a critical 99% Recall for the 'No Tumor' class.
models_and_performance.secondary_explainer: Random Forest (150 estimators). Achieved 83.19% Test Accuracy. Used specifically to extract spatial feature importances for Classical Saliency Heatmaps.
models_and_performance.dl_comparison: CNN architectures were tested but discarded. They suffered from severe spatial bias, learning skull artefacts rather than tumor tissue due to the small dataset size.
